title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Japp and Hercule Poirot question Miss Molly George regarding potential motives for her brother Henry Morley's death. Miss Molly identifies Frank Carter, an unpleasant man recently laid off from an insurance company, as a person of interest. She notes that Glady Neville had lent Carter some of her savings and that Henry had previously attempted to convince Glady to end her engagement with him. Miss Molly also mentions that Henry's partner, Mr. Reilly, is an Irishman with a drinking problem and a tendency to argue about politics, which Henry found unprofessional. Furthermore, Miss Molly reveals that Henry's will leaves the bulk of his estate to her, providing only £100 to Glady.

Glady Neville arrives in an agitated state, explaining that she received a telegram claiming her aunt had suffered a stroke. She dismisses the possibility that Carter sent the telegram as a prank. Glady provides two lists: one for patients seen by Mr. Morley and another for Mr. Reilly. On the latter list, Poirot identifies Colonel Abercrombie as a person of interest. Japp expresses interest in interviewing Mr. Ambrosio, the last person recorded seeing Morley alive.

Poirot questions Japp's involvement, noting that Japp does not typically handle suicide cases. Japp explains he was nearby investigating a fraud case involving Alistair Blunt, a high-profile banker. Because Blunt requires protection from political extremists, such as communists or fascists, Scotland Yard is investigating the matter thoroughly. Poirot concludes that the incident may have been a failed attempt on Blunt's life and suggests a larger conspiracy involving significant financial interests.
